---
title: "Advanced Project 1: Epidemic Modeling and Inference"
---

Summer 2026 advanced pathway participants will use epidemic models as a context for learning how deterministic simulation, stochastic models, and Bayesian inference fit together.

## Three-Day Plan

::: {.schedule-list}
::: {.schedule-item}
### Day 1: Python Foundations for Modeling and Inference

Set up Python habits needed for the rest of the project: variables, functions, arrays, plotting, loops, random simulation, transition matrices, and likelihood intuition.

[Day 1 notebook](materials/Day1_revised.ipynb)
:::

::: {.schedule-item}
### Day 2: SIR Models, Euler's Method, and Markov Chains

Move from repeated update rules to epidemic models and stochastic transitions.

[Day 2 notebook](materials/Day2_revised_realdata_executed.ipynb)  
:::

::: {.schedule-item}
### Day 3: MCMC and Inference for State Space Models

Use simulation and probability ideas to estimate unknown model parameters from noisy observations.

[Day 3 notebook](materials/Day3_revised_realdata_executed.ipynb)
:::
:::

## Additional Project Materials

These files are kept for continuity with existing project materials and can be reorganized as the project grows.

- [Introduction to Markov Chains](materials/markov_chains.ipynb)
- [Introduction to MCMC](materials/MCMC.ipynb)
- [Fictitious data CSV](fictious_data.csv)

## Future Advanced Projects

The advanced pathway is expected to include about five projects over time. This page is structured so additional project pages can be added without changing the overall site design.
